name: Harmony Biosciences Holdings, Inc.
business: Harmony Biosciences markets WAKIX (pitolisant), a first-in-class therapy for narcolepsy, and is developing pipeline candidates for rare neurological diseases including idiopathic hypersomnia, Prader-Willi syndrome, myotonic dystrophy type 1, and rare epilepsies. WAKIX is the only FDA-approved narcolepsy treatment not classified as a controlled substance by the DEA. The company sells WAKIX to specialty distributors and pharmacies, which in turn dispense to patients.
namedSuppliers: Pharma Packaging Solutions, LLC dba Tjoapack, LLC

exposures.sentence: Our lead product, WAKIX (pitolisant) ( WAKIX ), is a first-in-class therapy with a novel mechanism of action designed to enhance histamine signaling in the brain by binding to H 3 receptors.

We are focusing our development efforts on other rare neurological disorders in which EDS is a prominent symptom, including Prader-Willi Syndrome ( PWS ) and myotonic dystrophy type 1, otherwise known as dystrophia myotonica ( DM1 ).
We have made progress in the development of two new formulations of pitolisant: pitolisant GR and pitolisant HD.
In April 2024, we entered into the Sublicense with Bioprojet for BP1.15205, an orexin 2 receptor agonist for the treatment of narcolepsy and other potential indications.
EPX-100 is currently in two Phase 3 registrational clinical trials, one for each of DS (the ARGUS Study) and LGS (the LIGHTHOUSE Study).

catalysts.event: FDA approval of WAKIX for cataplexy in pediatric patients six years and older
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the FDA approved WAKIX for the treatment of cataplexy in pediatric patients six years and older with narcolepsy.

catalysts.event: Topline data from ONSTRIDE1 (narcolepsy) and ONSTRIDE2 (IH) Pitolisant HD Phase 3 registrational studies
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate topline data from both studies in 2027 and a potential PDUFA date for the Pitolisant HD narcolepsy and IH programs in 2028.
We are on track to submit a New Drug Application ( NDA ) for Pitolisant GR in early 2026 with a target PDUFA date in the first quarter of 2027.

catalysts.event: Topline data from BP1.15205 first-in-human study
catalysts.sentence: We filed an investigational medicinal product dossier ( IMPD ) with the EMA and began first-in-human studies in the fourth quarter of 2025 and are on-track to receive clinical PK data in mid-2026.

tamClaims.claim: The U.S. narcolepsy market had an approximate net sales value of $3.1 billion in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. narcolepsy market had an approximate net sales value of $3.1 billion in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 80,000 patients with IH in the U.S., with approximately 40,000 diagnosed.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 80,000 patients with IH in the U.S., with approximately 40,000 diagnosed.

tamClaims.claim: It is estimated that approximately 15,000 to 20,000 people in the United States suffer from PWS.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that approximately 15,000 to 20,000 people in the United States suffer from PWS.

tamClaims.claim: DM1 affects as many as 160,000 patients in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1 DM1, otherwise known as dystrophia myotonica, is the most common form of adult-onset muscular dystrophy and affects as many as 160,000 patients in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable market for EPX-100 in DS is approximately 5,000 people based on estimated current DS prevalence of approximately 8,600 cases and approximately 7,000 diagnosed DS cases.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe the total addressable market for EPX-100 among patients with DS is approximately 5,000 people based on an estimated current DS prevalence of approximately 8,600 cases and approximately 7,000 diagnosed DS cases.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable market for EPX-100 in LGS is approximately 35,000 people based on estimated current LGS prevalence of approximately 48,000 cases and 44,000 diagnosed LGS cases.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe the total addressable market for EPX-100 among patients with LGS is approximately 35,000 people based on an estimated current LGS prevalence of approximately 48,000 cases and 44,000 diagnosed LGS cases.
